prompt: Research SOTA shareable Trust Center / security-evidence-sharing products in 2026 (SafeBase, Whistic, Vanta Trust Center, Conveyor, Drata Trust Center). kolm gives each customer a shareable signed Trust link their buyer's reviewer verifies offline. What features make a shared trust/evidence page CREDIBLE and REUSABLE by enterprise reviewers: access control / email-gating / NDA click-through, auto-updating evidence, framework crosswalk display, document vault, questionnaire auto-answer, audit trail of who viewed. Cite specifics. Conclude with what kolm's Trust link page should add beyond rendering a signed report to match SOTA trust centers.
content:
**SOTA shareable Trust Centers in 2026 (primarily SafeBase/Drata, Whistic, Vanta, Conveyor, and Drata's own Trust Center) emphasize gated, self-serve portals that balance transparency with security controls.** These platforms enable vendors to proactively share compliance artifacts, deflect repetitive questionnaires, and provide reusable evidence for enterprise buyer reviews. SafeBase (acquired by Drata and often branded as Drata Trust Center powered by SafeBase) pioneered much of the category and remains a leader, alongside integrated offerings from compliance platforms like Vanta and dedicated tools like Conveyor and Whistic.[[1]](https://1up.ai/blog/trust-center-products)[[2]](https://www.vanta.com/resources/best-trust-center-software)

Key platforms and their positioning (based on 2025–2026 analyses):
- **Drata/SafeBase Trust Center**: Focuses on a buyer-friendly portal with secure document sharing, request gating/approvals, branding, auto-sync of compliance docs, magic links, request tracking, audit logs, Trust Library for reusable responses, and native AI questionnaire assistance. Strong for SMBs/scaling teams and enterprise use (e.g., customers like OpenAI, LinkedIn). Features include searchable buyer experiences and granular document controls.[[3]](https://drata.com/products/trust-center)[[1]](https://1up.ai/blog/trust-center-products)
- **Whistic Trust Center**: Emphasizes a centralized, customizable portal for sharing security/compliance info, with access controls, tracking of users/views/requests, NDA safeguards (including domain-level or uploaded NDAs and Docusign integration), and a Trust Center Exchange for reusing verified vendor evidence across a network. Includes badge display for certs and questionnaire libraries.[[4]](https://www.whistic.com/)[[5]](https://www.whistic.com/resources/blog/5-tips-for-making-a-great-trust-center)
- **Vanta Trust Center**: Branded security portal with real-time/continuous controls monitoring, AI-powered chatbot for buyer questions, customization/branding, analytics on engagement/ROI, unlimited views, NDA workflows (including Docusign sync), subscriptions to updates, and public/private links. Integrates with Vanta's compliance automation for live posture views.[[6]](https://www.vanta.com/products/trust-center)[[7]](https://www.vanta.com/resources/introducing-vanta-trust-center-state-of-trust-report)
- **Conveyor Trust Center**: Highlights secure/branded document sharing, watermarking, access controls, analytics (including revenue impact), and strong AI for answering entire questionnaires or DDQs via its Trust Center Agent. Supports gated portals and self-serve experiences.[[8]](https://www.conveyor.com/platform/trust-center)[[9]](https://skypher.co/post/skypher-vs-conveyor-what-is-the-best-security-questionnaire-automation-tool)
- **Drata Trust Center (standalone or via SafeBase)**: Overlaps heavily with SafeBase capabilities, adding clickwrap NDA support, live security posture views, Trust Library for consistent responses, access request/approval workflows, and document sharing controls.[[10]](https://help.drata.com/en/articles/8067402-trust-center-essential-and-pro-plans)

These platforms are frequently compared in 2026 guides as top options, with SafeBase/Drata, Vanta, and Conveyor often ranking highest for dedicated trust-center experiences.[[2]](https://www.vanta.com/resources/best-trust-center-software)[[11]](https://www.complyjet.com/blog/best-trust-center-software)

**Features that make a shared trust/evidence page credible and reusable by enterprise reviewers** (directly addressing the listed capabilities, with platform examples):

- **Access control / email-gating / NDA click-through**: Essential for credibility, as it prevents broad public exposure while enabling controlled sharing. Examples include magic links or gated access (Drata/SafeBase), request-and-approval workflows with private document access (Drata), clickwrap/click-to-sign NDAs or NDA uploads with domain-level persistence (Drata/SafeBase, Vanta, Whistic), and email-based or pre-approved access. Whistic and others track NDA status across tools like Salesforce. This allows reviewers to access sensitive items (e.g., full SOC 2 reports) without manual legal back-and-forth.[[12]](https://drata.com/learn/assurance/trust-center-design)[[13]](https://www.cyberbase.ai/blog/what-is-a-trust-center-2026)[[14]](https://whistichelp.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/14767501524119-Setting-Up-Your-Whistic-Trust-Center)

- **Auto-updating evidence**: Builds trust through freshness and reduces staleness concerns. Platforms auto-sync docs from compliance systems (Drata/SafeBase), provide live/real-time views of security posture and controls (Drata/Vanta), or enable subscriptions to updates (Vanta). This ensures evidence reflects current attestations without manual refreshes.[[1]](https://1up.ai/blog/trust-center-products)[[6]](https://www.vanta.com/products/trust-center)

- **Framework crosswalk display**: Enhances reusability by mapping evidence to multiple standards at a glance. Common via compliance badges/sections (SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, etc.), certifications/attestations lists, and control mappings or knowledge bases (all major platforms; e.g., Vanta/Drata compliance showcases and Whistic badge displays).[[15]](https://drata.com/learn/assurance/trust-center-overview)

- **Document vault**: Acts as a secure, tiered repository (public vs. gated vs. private). Features include granular sharing controls, bulk downloads, watermarking on PDFs, request-based access, and centralized hosting (Drata/SafeBase, Conveyor, Whistic). This supports self-serve while protecting sensitive assets like pen-test reports or architecture diagrams.[[3]](https://drata.com/products/trust-center)[[9]](https://skypher.co/post/skypher-vs-conveyor-what-is-the-best-security-questionnaire-automation-tool)

- **Questionnaire auto-answer**: Dramatically improves reusability by reducing repetitive requests. AI-powered features draft responses from Trust Center content/documents/past answers (Drata/SafeBase AI assistance, Conveyor Trust Center Agent for full questionnaires, Vanta chatbot). Reusable Trust Libraries or question banks further enable this.[[3]](https://drata.com/products/trust-center)[[2]](https://www.vanta.com/resources/best-trust-center-software)

- **Audit trail of who viewed**: Critical for accountability and compliance (e.g., proving access controls). Includes viewer analytics/engagement tracking, access logs, download tracking, request histories, and full audit trails (Drata/SafeBase, Whistic, Conveyor, Vanta). Reviewers and vendors can see exactly who accessed what and when.[[1]](https://1up.ai/blog/trust-center-products)[[5]](https://www.whistic.com/resources/blog/5-tips-for-making-a-great-trust-center)[[11]](https://www.complyjet.com/blog/best-trust-center-software)

Additional credibility boosters seen across SOTA platforms include searchable interfaces, branding/custom domains/subdomains, FAQs/knowledge bases, embedded pages for websites, revenue/ROI attribution analytics, and integrations (e.g., Salesforce, Docusign).

**For kolm's Trust link page (a shareable signed Trust link verified offline by the buyer's reviewer), to match SOTA it should add beyond static signed-report rendering**: Interactive/gated access (email verification, magic links, or click-through NDA for sensitive layers); a document vault with tiered controls, watermarking, and bulk options; auto-updating or live evidence syncing where possible; framework mappings/badges and crosswalks; AI-assisted or reusable questionnaire answers drawn from the content; and viewer/access audit trails/analytics. Optional enhancements for full parity include searchable knowledge bases/FAQs, branding options, update subscriptions, and request/approval workflows. This would transform the link from a one-time verifiable artifact into a reusable, credible, self-serve enterprise review hub while preserving the offline signature verification core.[[13]](https://www.cyberbase.ai/blog/what-is-a-trust-center-2026)

These capabilities collectively reduce friction in sales/security reviews, accelerate deals, and provide defensible transparency.
